input.prompt:
I'm worried these tests might have problems. Audit
`Payments.Tests/PaymentProcessorTests.cs` for .NET test anti-patterns
using the full anti-pattern catalog (missing assertions, swallowed
exceptions, always-true/self-comparing assertions, flakiness, magic
values, duplicate tests, broad exception types, etc.) and tell me
what (if anything) genuinely needs fixing — be careful not to invent
issues that aren't really there.

Give me a severity-ranked list (Critical / Warning / Info / "no
issues found"). Each finding must be tied to a named anti-pattern
from the catalog and include a concrete code-level fix. Do not edit
the files — this is a read-only review.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to audit a test file for .NET test anti-patterns. Let me read the file first.

data.content:
## Audit Result: **No issues found** ✅

This is a well-written test class. Here's why none of the common anti-patterns apply:

| Anti-pattern checked | Verdict |
|---|---|
| Missing assertions | ❌ Every test has meaningful assertions on the result |
| Swallowed exceptions | ❌ `Assert.ThrowsException` properly verifies the exception type |
| Always-true / self-comparing assertions | ❌ All assertions compare against expected literals |
| Broad exception types (catching `Exception`) | ❌ Tests assert the specific `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` |
| Magic values without context | ❌ Values are simple, contextually obvious amounts/order IDs |
| Duplicate / redundant tests | ❌ Zero and negative are distinct boundary cases |
| Flakiness (shared state, time-dependency, network) | ❌ Uses a `FakeGateway`; no shared mutable state |
| Test logic in production code | ❌ Not present |
| Non-determinism | ❌ All inputs are fixed literals |

The tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert, use parameterized data rows for currency variants, assert specific exception types, and isolate the SUT with a fake dependency. Nothing here needs fixing.
